replies.author: @FlyingHogs
replies.text:
@Joshua-d9x I’d guess not for the next few years. I think most people had that mindset with the new Star Wars trilogy, and were burnt because of it. Same with LotR. And with Marvel. And soon to be with Harry Potter. I’m a guy who lived and breathed Star Wars, lord of the rings, marvel, HP, Percy Jackson, Wheel of Time, etc. We are where we are now though. In the future, it will be studied in depth how multibillion dollar industries bought popular IPs and managed to evaporate fan bases that numbered in the tens or hundreds of millions to a small fraction of their original base.
Do you still not understand how they took predominately male IPs and tried to double their audience and fail spectacularly? There’s an analogy: trying to hit two birds with one stone.
It’d be like taking Barbie franchise and making turning it into a Ken action flick. It somehow passes through the Boardroom because in theory it might work, thereby doubling profits. In practice, it’s a really dumb idea.
Another analogy: fly too close to the sun and your wings melt.
They had a fan base of millions. They wanted more. Now they get less.
We are pattern recognition seeking animals. It would be stupid at this point to NOT judge the movie before watching it. Pattern recognition. The ability to learn when to stop wasting your time.
